```{r plot-module-trait-heatmap, fig.width=10, fig.height=12}
dim(textMatrix) <- dim(moduleTraitCor)

# Plot
par(mar = c(6, 8.5, 3, 3))
labeledHeatmap(
  Matrix        = moduleTraitCor,
  xLabels       = c("Lead sensitivity\n(mean β)", "Tau effect\n(mean τ)", "Sig. vs baseline"),
  yLabels       = names(MEs),
  ySymbols      = names(MEs),
  colorLabels   = FALSE,
  colors        = blueWhiteRed(50),
  textMatrix    = textMatrix,
  setStdMargins = FALSE,
  cex.text      = 0.5,
  zlim          = c(-1, 1),
  main          = "Module-trait relationships"
)
dev.off()

par(mar = c(6, 8.5, 3, 3))
labeledHeatmap(
  Matrix      = moduleTraitCor,
  xLabels     = c("Lead sensitivity\n(mean β)", "Tau effect\n(mean τ)", "Sig. vs baseline"),
  yLabels     = names(MEs),
  ySymbols    = names(MEs),
  colorLabels = FALSE,
  colors      = blueWhiteRed(50),
  textMatrix  = textMatrix,
  setStdMargins = FALSE,
  cex.text    = 0.5,
  zlim        = c(-1, 1),
  main        = "Module-trait relationships"
)
```

## 10.7 Identifying Hub Genes in Significant Modules
```{r hub-genes}
# Identify modules significantly correlated with lead sensitivity (mean_beta)
# Use FDR-corrected p-values across all module-trait pairs
sig_modules <- data.frame(
  module  = rownames(moduleTraitCor),
  r       = moduleTraitCor[, "mean_beta"],
  p       = moduleTraitPval[, "mean_beta"]
) %>%
  mutate(
    padj      = p.adjust(p, method = "BH"),
    direction = ifelse(r > 0, "Sensitive", "Resistant")
  ) %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj)

print(sig_modules)

# For each significant module, extract the top hub genes —
# genes with the highest module membership (kME), which is the
# correlation of a gene's expression with the module eigengene.
# Hub genes are the most central and likely most functionally important
# members of the module.
kME <- signedKME(expr_mat_t, MEs, outputColumnName = "kME")

hub_gene_list <- list()

for (mod in sig_modules$module) {
  mod_color <- gsub("^ME", "", mod)
  mod_genes <- names(net$colors)[net$colors == mod_color]
  kme_col   <- paste0("kME", mod_color)
  
  hub_genes <- data.frame(
    gene_id = mod_genes,
    kME     = kME[mod_genes, kme_col]
  ) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(kME))) %>%
    slice_head(n = 20) %>%
    left_join(ensg_to_symbol %>% select(gene_id, gene_name), by = "gene_id")
  
  hub_gene_list[[mod_color]] <- hub_genes
  message(sprintf("\nTop hub genes — %s module (r = %.2f, padj = %.3f):",
                  mod_color, sig_modules$r[sig_modules$module == mod],
                  sig_modules$padj[sig_modules$module == mod]))
  print(hub_genes %>% select(gene_name, kME))
}

# Save hub gene tables
hub_gene_df <- bind_rows(hub_gene_list, .id = "module")
write_csv(hub_gene_df, here("Analysis_Outputs", "10-7_wgcna_hub_genes.csv"))
```

```{r wgcna-enrichment, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Run GSEA on hub genes for each significant module using kME as the ranking
# statistic — genes with highest kME are most central to the module
module_gsea_results <- list()

for (mod_color in names(hub_gene_list)) {
  
  r_beta        <- sig_modules$r[sig_modules$module == paste0("ME", mod_color)]
  kme_col       <- paste0("kME", mod_color)
  all_mod_genes <- names(net$colors)[net$colors == mod_color]
  
  # Annotate hub genes with direction relative to lead sensitivity
  # sign(kME) * sign(r_beta): +1 = high in sensitive, -1 = high in resistant
  hub_gene_list[[mod_color]] <- hub_gene_list[[mod_color]] %>%
    mutate(
      direction_vs_sensitivity = case_when(
        sign(kME) * sign(r_beta) > 0 ~ "High in sensitive donors",
        sign(kME) * sign(r_beta) < 0 ~ "High in resistant donors",
        TRUE ~ "neutral"
      )
    )
  
  # Build ranked list corrected for sensitivity direction:
  # positive rank = associated with lead sensitivity
  # negative rank = associated with lead resistance
  ranked <- kME[all_mod_genes, kme_col] * sign(r_beta)
  names(ranked) <- all_mod_genes
  ranked <- sort(ranked, decreasing = TRUE)
  ranked <- ranked[!is.na(ranked)]
  
  # Run GSEA against Reactome
  set.seed(42)
  gsea_res <- fgseaMultilevel(
    pathways = reactome.list,
    stats    = ranked,
    minSize  = 10,
    maxSize  = 500
  )
  
  module_gsea_results[[mod_color]] <- gsea_res %>%
    filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
    arrange(padj)
  
  message(sprintf("\n--- %s module (r_beta = %.2f, %s) --- %d significant pathways",
                  mod_color, r_beta,
                  sig_modules$direction[sig_modules$module == paste0("ME", mod_color)],
                  nrow(module_gsea_results[[mod_color]])))
  
  print(module_gsea_results[[mod_color]] %>%
          select(pathway, NES, padj) %>%
          head(15))
}

# Print hub gene directionality summary for top modules
for (mod_color in c("greenyellow", "brown", "red")) {
  r_beta <- sig_modules$r[sig_modules$module == paste0("ME", mod_color)]
  message(sprintf("\n--- ME%s hub genes (r_beta = %.2f) ---", mod_color, r_beta))
  print(
    hub_gene_list[[mod_color]] %>%
      select(gene_name, kME, direction_vs_sensitivity) %>%
      head(20)
  )
}

# Save results
saveRDS(module_gsea_results,
        file.path(output_dir, "10-7_wgcna_module_gsea.rds"))
write_csv(
  bind_rows(module_gsea_results, .id = "module"),
  here("Analysis_Outputs", "10-7_wgcna_module_pathway_enrichment.csv")
)

# Lollipop plots for top three modules
# Positive NES = enriched in sensitive donors
# Negative NES = enriched in resistant donors
for (mod_color in c("greenyellow", "brown", "red")) {
  
  if (nrow(module_gsea_results[[mod_color]]) == 0) {
    message(sprintf("No significant pathways for %s module", mod_color))
    next
  }
  
  direction_label <- sig_modules$direction[
    sig_modules$module == paste0("ME", mod_color)]
  r_beta <- sig_modules$r[sig_modules$module == paste0("ME", mod_color)]
  padj   <- sig_modules$padj[sig_modules$module == paste0("ME", mod_color)]
  
  top_up   <- module_gsea_results[[mod_color]] %>%
    filter(NES > 0) %>% slice_min(padj, n = 10)
  top_down <- module_gsea_results[[mod_color]] %>%
    filter(NES < 0) %>% slice_min(padj, n = 10)
  top_paths <- bind_rows(top_up, top_down)
  
  if (nrow(top_paths) == 0) next
  
  top_paths <- top_paths %>%
    mutate(
      pathway = gsub("REACTOME_", "", pathway),
      pathway = gsub("_", " ", pathway),
      pathway = str_to_title(pathway)
    )
  
  p <- ggplot(top_paths, aes(x = reorder(pathway, NES), y = NES)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = reorder(pathway, NES), yend = 0),
                 color = "gray60") +
    geom_point(aes(fill = NES > 0), pch = 21, size = 4, stroke = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("TRUE"  = "#cc2132", "FALSE" = "#276bc4"),
      labels = c("TRUE"  = "High in sensitive donors",
                 "FALSE" = "High in resistant donors"),
      name   = NULL
    ) +
    coord_flip() +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA)) +
    labs(
      x        = NULL,
      y        = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
      title    = sprintf("ME%s module — Reactome pathway enrichment", mod_color),
      subtitle = sprintf("Lead %s module  |  r = %.2f, padj = %.2e",
                         direction_label, r_beta, padj)
    )
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    here("Analysis_Outputs",
         sprintf("10-7_wgcna_pathways_%s_module.png", mod_color)),
    plot   = p,
    width  = 11,
    height = 7,
    dpi    = 300,
    bg     = "white"
  )
}
```

```{r}
# Merge eigengenes with trait data and donor info for plotting
ME_wide <- MEs %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample_id") %>%
  mutate(donor = extracted_donors) %>%
  left_join(townlet %>% select(donor, mean_beta, sig_vs_baseline),
            by = "donor")

# Verify mean_beta is here before pivoting
head(ME_wide %>% select(sample_id, donor, mean_beta))

# Step 2: pivot only the ME columns
ME_plot_data <- ME_wide %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = matches("^ME[a-z]"),
    names_to  = "module",
    values_to = "eigengene"
  ) %>%
  filter(module %in% sig_modules$module) %>%
  left_join(sig_modules %>% select(module, r, padj, direction),
            by = "module") %>%
  # join mean_beta back in from ME_wide after pivoting
  left_join(ME_wide %>% select(sample_id, mean_beta), by = "sample_id") %>%
  mutate(
    module_label = sprintf("%s\nr = %.2f, padj = %.2e", module, r, padj),
    module_label = factor(module_label,
                          levels = unique(module_label[order(abs(r),
                                                             decreasing = TRUE)]))
  )

head(ME_plot_data %>% select(sample_id, donor, mean_beta, eigengene, module))
ggplot(ME_plot_data, aes(x = eigengene, y = mean_beta)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = sig_vs_baseline), alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
              linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "#cc2132", "FALSE" = "grey60"),
    labels = c("TRUE" = "Sig. vs baseline", "FALSE" = "Not significant"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ module_label, scales = "free_x", ncol = 4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40",
             linewidth = 0.4) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
    panel.border     = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA)
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = "Module eigengene expression",
    y     = "Lead sensitivity (mean β)",
    title = "Module eigengene vs lead sensitivity per pseudosample",
    subtitle = "Each point is one donor-treatment pseudosample (n=3 per donor) | Red = donor significantly responsive vs baseline"

  )

ggsave(here("Analysis_Outputs", "10-7_wgcna_eigengene_vs_sensitivity.png"),
       width = 16, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

```





```{r save-wgcna-objects}
# Save all WGCNA outputs for reproducibility
saveRDS(net,             file.path(output_dir, "10-wgcna_network.rds"))
saveRDS(MEs,             file.path(output_dir, "10-wgcna_eigengenes.rds"))
saveRDS(moduleTraitCor,  file.path(output_dir, "10-wgcna_module_trait_cor.rds"))
write_csv(sig_modules,   here("Analysis_Outputs", "10-6_wgcna_significant_modules.csv"))

message("WGCNA analysis complete. Key outputs saved to Analysis_Outputs/.")
```




# Session Information

Recording your session information is a simple but important step for
reproducibility. It captures the exact R and package versions used,
allowing others (and your future self) to reproduce this analysis.

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
